text.title: perf-test-tools Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for perf-test-tools. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Always run a 30-second smoke test at 5 VUs before any real load test. If smoke fails, fix the script before touching the system under test.
rule.rationale: Agents that skip smoke tests waste 30 minutes of real load runs on a broken script. Smoke at 5 VUs confirms authentication works, scenarios parse, and all HTTP routes respond — the minimum correctness check.

rule.statement: In k6, always export raw JSON (--out json=results.json) for trend analysis; HTML reports are throwaway. Pin tool version in CI (grafana/k6:0.50.0) — minor versions change metric names.
rule.rationale: JSON output enables diffing runs across commits. Unpinned versions produce metric name mismatches that silently break threshold checks.

rule.statement: k6 is JavaScript but does not support Node.js APIs. Do not import fs, axios, or node built-ins — they don't exist in k6's runtime.
rule.rationale: Agents hallucinate k6 module names (k6/http2 doesn't exist). Run k6 inspect script.js before any real run to catch import errors.

rule.statement: Use standard threshold pattern: http_req_duration p(95) < 500ms, http_req_failed rate < 1%, custom errorRate < 5%.
rule.rationale: These thresholds should be validated over 3+ baseline runs before adding to CI gate. Too-tight thresholds cause flakes that erode trust.

rule.statement: Use standard stage pattern: 2m ramp to 50 VUs, 5m hold, 2m ramp to 100 VUs, 5m hold, 2m ramp down.
rule.rationale: Staged ramps warm up the system gradually and reveal capacity at different load levels.

rule.statement: In Locust, always use self.client for HTTP calls — never use the requests library directly. Calls via requests are invisible to Locust's measurement layer.
rule.rationale: Invisible requests produce false-negative results: the scenario runs but Locust shows no requests in the dashboard or output.
Headless mode is appropriate for CI; web UI is for interactive exploration.

rule.statement: Use @task(N) weight to model realistic user behavior (browse 10x more than checkout 1x).
rule.rationale: Weighted tasks produce realistic request distributions that match production traffic patterns.

rule.statement: Always include realistic think time in scenarios: sleep(random(1, 4)) in k6, between(1, 5) in Locust. Without think time, you benchmark the load generator, not the SUT.
rule.rationale: Real users don't fire requests as fast as possible. Scenarios without think time produce artificially high request rates that break before revealing actual capacity limits.

rule.statement: Always smoke-test the generated script first. Agents that go straight to 1000 VUs waste cloud spend when the scenario is broken.
rule.rationale: A broken scenario at 1000 VUs costs real money and time. A smoke at 5 VUs costs 30 seconds.

rule.statement: Review the generated scenario weights and stage shape before running any test lasting more than 5 minutes. Cloud spend accumulates quickly at 100+ VUs.
rule.rationale: An agent that starts a 30-minute k6 cloud run with wrong weights or wrong target URL burns budget and produces useless data. The 30-second review before the run is the cheapest gate.

rule.statement: Keep soak tests (8h, low load) and stress tests (3 min, ramp to break) in separate files. Never combine into one scenario.
rule.rationale: Agents conflate them. Soak finds memory leaks and connection pool exhaustion; stress finds throughput ceiling. They require different VU counts, durations, and thresholds.

rule.statement: Run a 30-second smoke at 5 VUs before any real test. If smoke fails, fix the script — not the SUT.
rule.rationale: Smoke testing is the minimal correctness check and prevents wasted time on broken scenarios.

rule.statement: Always export raw JSON (--out json=) for diffing across runs; HTML reports are throwaway.
rule.rationale: JSON enables regression detection and trend analysis. HTML reports are used once and discarded.

rule.statement: Use realistic think time and keep-alive on; without them you're benchmarking the load generator.
rule.rationale: Real users have think time between requests; connection pooling is standard in production.

rule.statement: Separate "soak" (8h, low load) from "stress" (3 min, ramp to break) into distinct files; agents conflate them.
rule.rationale: Soak and stress test different failure modes and require different configurations.

rule.statement: Tag runs with git SHA + scenario name in metric labels — required for trend analysis.
rule.rationale: Trend analysis requires identifying which commit produced which results.

rule.statement: Pin tool versions in CI (grafana/k6:0.50.0) — k6 minor versions change metric names.
rule.rationale: Version drift causes silent metric name mismatches that break threshold checks.
